name: Choanoke Area Development Association Of Nc, Inc.
description_short: NC CADA is a non-profit community development organization serving five rural North Carolina counties with free housing counseling, Head Start programs, and emergency assistance.
description_long:
Choanoke Area Development Association of NC, Inc. (CADA) is a Community Action Agency established to serve low-income families and seniors across Bertie, Halifax, Hertford, Northampton, and Martin Counties in eastern North Carolina. The organization operates as a non-profit funded through Community Services Block Grant (CSBG) programs and partners with state and federal agencies including the North Carolina Housing Finance Agency and Department of Health and Human Services. CADA's mission centers on anti-poverty initiatives and self-sufficiency programs tailored to rural communities with limited access to financial services.

CADA offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost services including HUD-approved housing counseling through their Foreclosure Prevention and Homeownership Programs, Head Start and Early Head Start early education services, emergency food and shelter assistance, weatherization programs to reduce utility costs, employment services, and parenting programs. The organization also administers Housing Choice Voucher Programs and recently received $162,000 in essential single-family rehabilitation loan funding per county for qualifying homeowners. Their services target households below 80% of area median income, with specific support for elderly individuals, disabled residents, veterans, and families with young children.

CADA distinguishes itself through direct community presence via five county-based service centers staffed with local coordinators who understand regional challenges. Rather than operating as a commercial lender, CADA provides counseling and grants—not loans requiring repayment—making services genuinely accessible to the poorest households. The organization maintains transparent public hearing processes for program planning and publishes annual reports demonstrating accountability to the communities it serves.

The primary limitation is geographic scope: services are restricted to five rural North Carolina counties, making CADA unavailable to most consumers. Website navigation is difficult with broken links (404 errors on About pages), limiting information accessibility. Program capacity appears modest (3-6 housing rehabilitation loans per county annually), meaning waitlists or application windows are likely. The organization functions as a safety-net provider rather than a comprehensive financial services institution, so consumers needing broader lending products must seek alternative sources.
pros: Completely free housing counseling and foreclosure prevention services (HUD-approved)
No-repayment emergency assistance grants for food, shelter, and urgent repairs
Funded by federal CSBG and state programs—no predatory lending or high fees
County-based local offices with named coordinators providing personalized support
Specialized programs for vulnerable populations (elderly, disabled, veterans, low-income families)
Weatherization assistance to reduce utility bills for eligible households
Head Start/Early Head Start early education programs for children under five
cons: Geographic limitation: serves only five rural North Carolina counties
Website has broken links and poor navigation, making it difficult to access full program details
Limited program capacity (e.g., 3-6 housing rehabilitation loans per county annually suggests long wait times)
Eligibility restricted to households below 80% of area median income—not accessible to middle-income consumers
No online application portal visible; applications require phone contact or in-person visits during specific windows
best_for: Low-income homeowners in rural NC counties facing foreclosure or needing home repairs
Families with young children seeking affordable early education through Head Start programs
Seniors and disabled residents needing emergency financial assistance and utility cost reduction
Veterans and elderly households qualifying for weatherization and homeownership counseling
diagnosis: NC CADA is best for low-income households in rural eastern North Carolina (Bertie, Halifax, Hertford, Northampton, Martin counties) seeking free, non-commercial housing counseling, emergency assistance, or early education services. The critical caveat is strict geographic and income restrictions: services are unavailable outside these five counties and limited to households below 80% of area median income, making it unsuitable for consumers outside this service territory or above income thresholds.
services: Essential Single Family Rehabilitation Loans (up to $162K per county, 2024-2026)
Head Start and Early Head Start programs
Weatherization assistance to improve home energy efficiency
Community Services Block Grant (CSBG) self-sufficiency programs
Early Head Start services for infants and toddlers
Public anti-poverty planning and community needs assessments
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
